original: Joey Chestnut sets a new world record by eating 83 hot dogs in Netflix's "Unfinished Beef" Labor Day Showdown
The first sci-fi film, "A Trip To The Moon," is released
Christa McAuliffe, American teacher who died in Challenger space shuttle disaster, born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 1986)
V-J Day, formal surrender of Japan signed aboard the USS Missouri, marks the end of World War II
India successfully launches its first spacecraft, Aditya-L1, to study the sun
Future US President George H. W. Bush bails out of a burning plane during a mission in the Pacific
John McClain performs 180 outside loops in an airplane over Houston
American sprinterWilma Rudolphwins the coveted 100m gold medal at the Rome Olympics in 11.0; beats Britain's Dorothy Hyman by 0.3; 1st of 3 gold medals for Rudolph
Great Fire of London begins at 2am in Pudding Lane, 80% of London is destroyed
Guy Laliberté, Canadian founder of Cirque du Soleil, born in Montreal, Quebec
The first automated teller machine in the United States is installed in Rockville Centre, New York
Solar super storm affects electrical telegraph service
First non-stop airplane flight from Europe to the US (37 hours)
Earth's trees number just over 3 trillion according to a study in "Nature" by Thomas Crowther of Yale University
A side of Rossberg Peak collapses into Goldau Valley Switzerland, kills 500
US PresidentBarack Obamabecomes the first president to visit the Arctic Circle at Kotzebue, Alaska
Theodore Rooseveltadvises "Speak softly and carry a big stick; you will go far" in an address to the Minnesota State Fair, entitled "National Duties"
The Principality of Sealand is established, ruled by Prince Paddy Roy Bates.
Rachael Gunn [Raygun], Australian breakdancer and academic (2024 Paris Olympics), born in Hornsby, Australia

Terry Bradshaw, College/Pro Football HOF quarterback (Louisiana Tech; Super Bowl 1974, 75, 78 [MVP], 79 [MVP]; NFL MVP 1978; First-team All-Pro 1978; 3 × Pro Bowl; Pittsburgh Steelers) and broadcaster (CBS, FOX), born in Shreveport, Louisiana
German Ferrari driverMichael Schumacherwins the Belgian Grand Prix at Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps for his record 52nd F1 Grand Prix victory
British boxerFrank Brunobeats American champion Oliver McCall in a unanimous 12 round points decision in London for the WBC heavyweight title
Toronto Blue Jays pitcher Dave Stieb throws MLB record 9th no-hitter of the season; beating Cleveland Indians, 3-0
Chicago Cubs sluggerErnie Bankshits his MLB 400th career HR (off Curt Simmons) in a 5-3 win over St. Louis at Wrigley Field; Simmons also gave up Willie Mays' 400th HR in 1963
American breaststroker John Hencken beats David Wilkie of Great Britain to win the 200m gold medal in world record 2:21.55 at the Munich Olympic Games
American Mike Troy swims 200m butterfly world record 2:12.8 to win the gold medal from Australian Neville Hayes at the Rome Olympics
Gavin Casalegno, American actor (The Summer I Turned Pretty, The Vampire Diaries), born in Dallas, Texas
Spencer Smith, American rock drummer (Panic! at the Disco), born in Denver, Colorado
Ishant Sharma, Indian cricket fast bowler (102 Tests, 306 wickets; 80 ODIs, 115 wickets; Sussex, Delhi Capitals), born in Delhi, India
Emiliano Martínez, Argentine football goalkeeper (Aston Villa, Argentina), born in Mar del Plata, Argentina
Eugenio Derbez, Mexican comedian and actor known for " nstructions Not Included", born in Mexico City, Mexico
Carlos Valderrama, Colombian footballer, born in Santa Marta, Colombia
Olivier Panis, French race car driver (1996 Monaco Grand Prix), born in Lyon, France
Ishmeet Singh [Sodhi], Indian playback singer (Voice of India, 2007), born in Ludhiana, Punjab, India (d. 2008)

SultanSaladinand KingRichard the Lionheartof England sign the Treaty of Jaffa ending the Third Crusade in a compromise neither is happy with. Treaty leaves Jerusalem under Islamic control with Christian pilgrimage rights and restores the Kingdom of Jerusalem.
Ho Chi Minhdeclares Vietnam independent from France (National Day)
Museum of Contemporary Art of Niterói (MAC) designed byOscar Niemeyerinaugurated in Niterói, Brazil
Dr Floyd J Lewis 1st uses deep freeze technique in heart surgery
First transatlantic "ping-pong" round-trip air flight from New York to England and back begins, piloted by Dick Merrill with Harry Richman
Great Smoky Mountains National Park dedicated
New Zealand beats Australia 14-3 in cold, wet conditions in the first international Rugby Union match between the countries on New Zealand soil at Tahuna Park in Dunedin

British Ferrari driver Peter Collins sportingly hands over his car to retired teammateJuan Manuel Fangioduring season ending Italian Grand Prix at Monza; Fangio finishes 2nd to win his 3rd straight F1 World Drivers Championship by 3 points from Englishman Stirling Moss
Yankees right fielderReggie Jacksonhits his 20th HR of the season in a 6-2 home win over Seattle; 19th MLB player to hit 20 HR in 11 straight years
US Men's Amateur Golf Championship, Canterbury GC:Mark O'Mearawins 8 & 7 over John Cook
Joe Auer returns the opening kickoff 95 yards for a Miami touchdown in the Dolphins' first ever NFL regular-season game; Oakland Raiders go on to win 23-14 at the Orange Bowl before 26,776 fans
Netherlands win their first men’s hockey World Cup, 4-2 on penalties over India in Amstelveen, Netherlands
Billy Martinis fired as Detroit Tigers manager due to continual differences with the front office; goes on to manage Texas, Oakland and NY Yankees (in 5 separate stints)
Trial begins in Moscow for West German pilot Mathias Rust, who flew a private plane from Finland to Moscow, USSR

James Forten, African-American abolitionist and businessman who used his wealth to push for civil rights and the abolition of slavery, born in Philadelphia, Province of Pennsylvania, British America (d. 1842)
Eugene Field "Poet of Childhood", American poet, writer and journalist (Little Boy Blue), born in St. Louis, Missouri (d. 1895)
Romare Bearden, African American painter and songwriter, born in Charlotte, North Carolina (d. 1988)
Ramón Valdés, Mexican actor and comedian (Don Ramón-El Chavo del Ocho), born in Mexico City (d. 1988)
Laurindo Almeida, Brazilian classical, jazz and Latin guitarist and composer (developed bossa nova), born in Prainha, São Paulo, Brazil (d. 1995)
Arthur Ashkin, American scientist (Nobel Prize for Physics 2018 Optical Tweezers), born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 2020)[1]
Trudi Jochum-Beiser, Austrian alpine skier (Olympic gold 1948, 52), born in Lech am Arlberg, Austria
Claude Nicollier, Swiss astronaut (STS 61-K, 46, 61, 75), born in Vevey, Switzerland
Sheila Bromberg, British orchestral and session harpist (The Beatles - "She's Leaving Home"; Heatwave -"Boogie Nights"), born in London (d. 2021)
John Thompson Jr., American Basketball HOF coach (Georgetown Uni 1972–99, NCAA Division I tournament 1984) and center (NBA C'ship 1965, 66 Boston Celtics), born in Washington, D.C. (d. 2020)
Rosalind Ashford, American rock vocalist (Martha Reeve & Vandellas - "Heat Wave"; "Dancing In The Street"), born in Detroit, Michigan
Michael Rother, German electronic musician (Neu!; Kraftwerk; Harmonia; Cluster), born in Hamburg, West Germany
